title: mentha × verticillata var. parviflora
intro: Mentha × verticillata var. parviflora, also known as American balm or wild bergamot mint, is a variety of mint plant native to North America, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and compact growth habit. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its essential oils and volatile compounds, which have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Mentha × verticillata var. parviflora include its ability to relieve digestive issues, soothe headaches and fever, and act as a natural insect repellent. The plant's essential oil can be extracted through steam distillation and used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, salves, and potpourri, to harness its medicinal and aromatic properties.
